source_rationale: RGS5 is a pericyte/perivascular marker highly expressed in DDX4 AB+ cells, suggesting a perivascular identity
TAGLN (transgelin/SM22) is a smooth muscle marker indicating contractile perivascular cell identity
ACTA2 (alpha smooth muscle actin) is a canonical marker of smooth muscle/pericyte lineage cells
NOTCH3 is a key receptor in pericyte/vascular smooth muscle cell specification and maintenance
PDGFRB is a canonical pericyte marker essential for pericyte recruitment and vascular development
MCAM (CD146) is a cell adhesion molecule expressed on pericytes and perivascular cells
MYH11 is a smooth muscle myosin heavy chain, a definitive marker of smooth muscle lineage
Phospholamban is expressed in smooth muscle cells and regulates calcium handling
MYL9 is a myosin regulatory light chain characteristic of smooth muscle/pericyte cells
GJA4 (connexin 37) is a gap junction protein expressed in perivascular and vascular cells
Decorin is a proteoglycan abundantly expressed in stromal/fibroblast cells of the ovary
PDGFRA is a canonical marker of mesenchymal stromal/fibroblast cells in the ovary
SERPINE2 is a serine protease inhibitor expressed in ovarian stromal cells
COL1A1 is a major collagen gene characteristic of fibroblast/stromal cell identity
FOXL2 is a key transcription factor in ovarian somatic cell differentiation and granulosa/stromal identity
STAR (steroidogenic acute regulatory protein) is essential for steroidogenesis in ovarian somatic cells
ARX is a transcription factor expressed in ovarian stromal/theca cells
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking ovarian stromal progenitor cells
CXCL12 is a chemokine produced by ovarian stromal cells important for follicle development
LHX9 is a LIM homeodomain transcription factor critical for gonadal somatic cell development
Von Willebrand factor is a canonical endothelial cell marker involved in hemostasis
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein specific to endothelial cells
VE-cadherin (CDH5) is a definitive endothelial-specific adherens junction protein
CD34 is a canonical marker of vascular endothelial cells and hematopoietic progenitors
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor and a key endothelial cell marker
Endomucin is a glycoprotein specifically expressed on vascular endothelial cells
ERG is an ETS transcription factor essential for endothelial cell identity and vascular development
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor specifically expressed on endothelial cells
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor important for vascular tube formation
ICAM2 is constitutively expressed on endothelial cells and used as an endothelial marker
SOX17 is a transcription factor critical for arterial endothelial cell specification
Anti-Müllerian hormone is a canonical marker of granulosa cells in growing follicles
FOXL2 is the master transcription factor for granulosa cell differentiation and maintenance
Inhibin alpha is produced by granulosa cells and is a key marker of granulosa cell function
Follistatin is expressed by granulosa cells and regulates activin signaling in folliculogenesis
AMH receptor type 2 is specifically expressed on granulosa cells mediating AMH signaling
17-beta hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase 1 is a steroidogenic enzyme characteristic of granulosa cells
Keratin 18 is an epithelial marker expressed in granulosa cells reflecting their epithelial nature
Prostaglandin D2 synthase is highly expressed in granulosa cells of the ovary
WFDC2 (HE4) is a secreted protein highly expressed in ovarian granulosa cells
WT1 is a transcription factor expressed in granulosa cells essential for ovarian development
Desmoplakin marks the epithelial character of granulosa cells with cell-cell junctions
TNNI3 is highly upregulated in granulosa cells and serves as a distinguishing marker
PTPRC (CD45) is the pan-leukocyte marker expressed on all immune cells
CD74 (invariant chain) is highly expressed on antigen-presenting immune cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein broadly expressed in myeloid and NK immune cells
AIF1 (IBA1) is a calcium-binding protein expressed in macrophages and microglia
CD52 is a glycoprotein broadly expressed on lymphocytes and monocytes
CXCR4 is a chemokine receptor broadly expressed on immune cells mediating homing
FCER1G is an Fc receptor gamma chain expressed on myeloid and NK immune cells
CD69 is an early activation marker expressed on activated lymphocytes and NK cells
Lysozyme is expressed by monocytes and macrophages as part of innate immunity
NKG7 is expressed in NK cells and cytotoxic T cells, marking cytotoxic immune populations
Coronin-1A is a leukocyte-specific protein essential for immune cell migration
CD14 is the canonical monocyte/macrophage marker and LPS co-receptor
CD163 is a scavenger receptor specific to monocytes and tissue-resident macrophages
C1QA is a complement component highly expressed in tissue macrophages
C1QB is a complement subunit marking tissue-resident macrophages
C1QC completes the C1Q complex characteristic of tissue macrophages
Lysozyme is a canonical monocyte/macrophage marker with antimicrobial function
CSF1R (CD115) is the M-CSF receptor essential for monocyte/macrophage differentiation
FOLR2 is a marker of tissue-resident macrophages, particularly in the ovary
MS4A7 is a membrane-spanning protein preferentially expressed on monocytes/macrophages
FCGR2A (CD32) is an Fc gamma receptor expressed on monocytes and macrophages
Cathepsin S is a cysteine protease highly expressed in monocytes/macrophages for antigen processing
LYVE1 marks a subset of tissue-resident macrophages associated with lymphatic vessels
MAFB is a transcription factor driving monocyte-to-macrophage differentiation
ZP3 is a zona pellucida glycoprotein exclusively expressed in oocytes
FIGLA is an oocyte-specific transcription factor essential for folliculogenesis
NLRP5 is a maternal-effect gene specifically expressed in oocytes
DPPA3 (Stella) is a germ cell-specific gene essential for oocyte development
ZAR1 is an oocyte-specific RNA-binding protein critical for early embryogenesis
LHX8 is a transcription factor essential for oocyte survival and follicle development
SOHLH2 is a germ cell-specific transcription factor required for oogenesis
SYCP3 is a synaptonemal complex protein marking meiotic germ cells/oocytes
TUBB8 is an oocyte-specific beta-tubulin essential for meiotic spindle assembly
GTSF1 is a germ cell-specific gene involved in piRNA pathway and transposon silencing
EPCAM marks germ cells/oocytes in the ovary distinguishing them from somatic cells
STAG3 is a meiosis-specific cohesin subunit essential for oocyte chromosome cohesion
PNLDC1 is involved in piRNA biogenesis and is specifically expressed in germ cells
RGS5 is the top marker of pericytes/perivascular cells regulating G-protein signaling in vasculature
Transgelin is a smooth muscle marker highly expressed in perivascular/mural cells
Alpha smooth muscle actin is a canonical marker of pericytes and vascular smooth muscle cells
Smooth muscle myosin heavy chain is a definitive marker of contractile perivascular cells
PDGFRB is the canonical pericyte receptor essential for pericyte recruitment to vessels
NOTCH3 is critical for vascular smooth muscle/pericyte cell fate and maintenance
MCAM (CD146) is a pericyte surface marker used for isolation and identification
Phospholamban regulates calcium in smooth muscle cells and marks perivascular identity
NDUFA4L2 is a hypoxia-responsive gene highly expressed in pericytes
MYL9 is a smooth muscle myosin light chain marking contractile perivascular cells
Calponin 1 is a smooth muscle-specific protein marking mature perivascular cells
Decorin is a hallmark extracellular matrix proteoglycan of stromal/fibroblast cells
PDGFRA is a canonical fibroblast/stromal cell marker in the ovary
Type I collagen alpha 1 is a major extracellular matrix gene defining stromal/fibroblast identity
Type I collagen alpha 2 chain is characteristic of collagen-producing stromal cells
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking ovarian stromal progenitor cells
FOXL2 is expressed in ovarian stromal cells and is essential for ovarian somatic cell identity
STAR marks steroidogenic stromal/theca cells in the ovary
ARX is a transcription factor expressed in ovarian theca/stromal cells
Osteoglycin is a proteoglycan characteristic of mesenchymal stromal cells
CXCL12 is a chemokine secreted by ovarian stromal cells supporting follicle niche
APOE is expressed in ovarian stromal cells involved in lipid metabolism and steroidogenesis
CD3D is a component of the T cell receptor complex and canonical T cell marker
CD3E is a T cell receptor signaling subunit defining T cell identity
CD3G completes the CD3 complex essential for T cell receptor signaling
CD8A marks cytotoxic T lymphocytes and is a canonical T cell subset marker
CCL5 (RANTES) is highly expressed by activated T cells and NK cells
Granzyme K is expressed by effector memory CD8+ T cells
IL7R (CD127) is a key marker of naive and memory T cells
CD2 is a T cell surface glycoprotein involved in T cell activation and adhesion
LCK is a T cell-specific tyrosine kinase essential for TCR signaling
PTPRC (CD45) is expressed on T cells and required for T cell receptor signaling
BCL11B is a transcription factor essential for T cell lineage commitment and identity
Granzyme A is a serine protease in cytotoxic T cells mediating cell killing
ITK is a T cell-specific kinase downstream of the T cell receptor
